batch_summary: At the coffee shop, Monica, Rachel, Ross, Chandler, and Joey discuss their fondness for Alan, though Monica wonders if he is "too much." Meanwhile, Phoebe meets her friend Lizzy on the street and generously gifts her $1,000. Lizzy is shocked and insists on giving something back, so they agree Lizzy will buy Phoebe a soda. Later, Chandler is shown at his office cubicle, secretly lighting a cigarette and spraying air freshener to hide the smoke while he works.
cumulative_summary: In a cozy coffee shop, Rachel, Monica, Ross, Chandler, and Joey reminisce about a recent softball game and praise a man named Alan for bringing them together as a team. While Monica tentatively questions if Alan is "too much," the guys enthusiastically defend him, expressing their deep admiration for his personality. Meanwhile, Phoebe meets up with a homeless woman named Lizzy on a city street. After bringing her soup, Phoebe surprises Lizzy by gifting her an envelope containing $1,000. Overwhelmed and reluctant to accept the charity for free, Lizzy tries to offer her tinfoil hat in return. Phoebe declines the hat but graciously agrees to let Lizzy buy her a soda so they can call it even. Later, the scene shifts to an office building where Chandler sits in his cubicle. Struggling with his urges, he sneaks a cigarette, lighting it at his desk and using air freshener to mask the smell while he continues to type on his computer.

batch_summary: After comically trying to hide his smoking at work, Chandler joins the group at the coffee shop. Meanwhile, Lizzy buys Phoebe a soda, but Phoebe is horrified to discover a severed thumb floating inside. When she shares this gross discovery with the group, the friends quickly shift to criticizing Chandler for smoking again. Defensively, Chandler deflects by pointing out everyone else's annoying habits—such as Joey's knuckle cracking and Monica's snort-laughing—leaving the group feeling self-conscious and tense.
